spec_match.rationale: Provides an interactive simulator modeling enforce vs report policies on custom script URLs and displays the simulated JSON reporting payloads.

interactive.rationale: Allows selecting the enforcement policy, dynamically adding script URLs with/without integrity, and outputting simulated reporting payloads immediately.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Directly describes the HTTP headers, blocked destinations, and the transition from report-only to enforce configurations.

edge_cases.rationale: Exposes both enforce, report, both (enforce + report), and none, representing all browser-applicable policy combinations.

references.rationale: Links correctly to the ChromeStatus entry `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5178394056327168` and the W3C Subresource Integrity spec.

design_system.rationale: Fully compliant with typography, layout margins, high-contrast colors, and grid layouts.

openQuestions.title: Support multiple destinations in simulation
openQuestions.detail: The simulation currently only targets the 'script' destination. Adding option flags to simulate other subresource destinations (like 'style', 'font', or 'image') under the same policy would reflect full spec coverage.

summary: An excellent baseline simulator that fully explains Integrity-Policy header mechanics, reporting scopes, and browser-side canceling behaviors with highly intuitive row additions.
